name: client
context: Build MCP clients that connect to servers, discover tools/resources/prompts, and invoke them on behalf of AI applications
info: This skill covers building MCP clients — the components inside AI applications that connect to MCP servers. Clients handle the initialization handshake, discover available primitives (tools, resources, prompts), invoke tools, read resources, and get prompts. The Python SDK provides ClientSession with stdio_client and streamablehttp_client transports. The TypeScript SDK provides Client with equivalent transports. Clients maintain a dedicated 1:1 session with each server. Most developers use existing host applications (Claude Desktop, VS Code) as clients, but building custom clients is needed for custom AI applications and agent frameworks.
body:
# Building MCP Clients

An MCP client is a component inside an AI application (the host) that connects to MCP servers. It manages the JSON-RPC session, discovers primitives, and invokes them.

Most developers use existing hosts (Claude Desktop, VS Code). Build a custom client when creating your own AI application or agent framework.

## Python Client

```python
import asyncio
from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters
from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client

async def main():
    server_params = StdioServerParameters(
        command="python",
        args=["server.py"]
    )

    async with stdio_client(server_params) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize()

            # Discover tools
            tools = await session.list_tools()
            print("Available tools:", [t.name for t in tools.tools])

            # Call a tool
            result = await session.call_tool("search", {"query": "hello"})
            print("Result:", result.content[0].text)

            # List resources
            resources = await session.list_resources()
            print("Resources:", [r.uri for r in resources.resources])

            # Read a resource
            content = await session.read_resource("schema://database")
            print("Schema:", content.contents[0].text)

            # List prompts
            prompts = await session.list_prompts()
            print("Prompts:", [p.name for p in prompts.prompts])

            # Get a prompt
            prompt = await session.get_prompt("review", {"code": "def foo(): pass"})
            print("Prompt:", prompt.messages[0].content.text)

asyncio.run(main())
```

## TypeScript Client

```typescript
import { Client } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js";
import { StdioClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js";

const transport = new StdioClientTransport({
  command: "python",
  args: ["server.py"]
});

const client = new Client({ name: "my-client", version: "1.0.0" });
await client.connect(transport);

// Discover tools
const tools = await client.listTools();
console.log("Tools:", tools.tools.map(t => t.name));

// Call a tool
const result = await client.callTool("search", { query: "hello" });
console.log("Result:", result.content[0].text);

// List and read resources
const resources = await client.listResources();
const content = await client.readResource("schema://database");

// List and get prompts
const prompts = await client.listPrompts();
const prompt = await client.getPrompt("review", { code: "function foo() {}" });
```

## Connecting to Remote Servers (Streamable HTTP)

```python
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client

async with streamablehttp_client("https://api.example.com/mcp") as (read, write, session_id):
    async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
        await session.initialize()
        # Use session as normal...
```

## Multi-Server Client

A host typically connects to multiple servers:

```python
class MCPHost:
    def __init__(self):
        self.sessions: dict[str, ClientSession] = {}

    async def connect(self, name: str, command: str, args: list[str]):
        params = StdioServerParameters(command=command, args=args)
        transport = await stdio_client(params).__aenter__()
        session = await ClientSession(*transport).__aenter__()
        await session.initialize()
        self.sessions[name] = session

    async def all_tools(self) -> list:
        tools = []
        for name, session in self.sessions.items():
            response = await session.list_tools()
            tools.extend(response.tools)
        return tools
```

## Handling Tool Calls with an LLM

The typical agentic loop: LLM selects tools, client executes them:

```python
# Get available tools and format for the LLM
tools_response = await session.list_tools()
llm_tools = [{
    "name": t.name,
    "description": t.description,
    "input_schema": t.inputSchema
} for t in tools_response.tools]

# LLM decides to use a tool
llm_response = anthropic.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    tools=llm_tools,
    messages=messages
)

# Execute tool calls
for block in llm_response.content:
    if block.type == "tool_use":
        result = await session.call_tool(block.name, block.input)
        # Feed result back to LLM
```

## Notification Handling

```python
# React to tool list changes
@session.on_notification("notifications/tools/list_changed")
async def on_tools_changed():
    tools = await session.list_tools()
    update_available_tools(tools)
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `MCP_SERVER_URL` | Remote server endpoint for HTTP transport |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | For LLM integration in the client app |

sections.title: Python MCP client
sections.body: Install `mcp` package. Use `stdio_client(StdioServerParameters(...))` for local or `streamablehttp_client(url)` for remote. Create `ClientSession(read, write)` and call `await session.initialize()`. Then use `list_tools()`, `call_tool()`, `list_resources()`, `read_resource()`, `list_prompts()`, `get_prompt()`.

sections.title: TypeScript MCP client
sections.body: Install `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`. Create `Client({name, version})` and connect with `StdioClientTransport` or HTTP transport. Use `client.listTools()`, `client.callTool()`, `client.listResources()`, `client.readResource()`, `client.listPrompts()`, `client.getPrompt()`.

sections.title: LLM tool integration loop
sections.body: The agentic pattern: 1) List tools from MCP server, 2) Format as LLM tool definitions, 3) Send to LLM with user message, 4) LLM returns tool_use blocks, 5) Execute via `call_tool()`, 6) Feed results back to LLM. This loop continues until the LLM stops requesting tools.

sections.title: Connecting to multiple servers
sections.body: A host creates one client per server. Each client maintains its own session. Aggregate tools/resources/prompts across all sessions to present a unified capability set to the LLM. Route tool calls to the correct session based on tool name.

scripts.name: Connect and List Tools
scripts.description: Connect to an MCP server via stdio and list all available tools
scripts.inline:
import asyncio
from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters
from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client
import sys
import json

async def main():
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python connect-and-list.py <server-command> [args...]")
        sys.exit(1)
    command = sys.argv[1]
    args = sys.argv[2:]
    params = StdioServerParameters(command=command, args=args)
    async with stdio_client(params) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            await session.initialize()
            tools = await session.list_tools()
            print("Tools:")
            for t in tools.tools:
                print(f"  {t.name}: {t.description}")
            resources = await session.list_resources()
            print("\nResources:")
            for r in resources.resources:
                print(f"  {r.uri}: {r.name}")
            prompts = await session.list_prompts()
            print("\nPrompts:")
            for p in prompts.prompts:
                print(f"  {p.name}: {p.description}")

asyncio.run(main())

serverCommand.description: Command to launch the MCP server

serverUrl.description: URL for remote Streamable HTTP server

depends.reason: Understanding the client role in MCP architecture
Clients must configure transport to connect to servers
